title: Hemigraphis crenata
intro: Hemigraphis crenata, also known as the silver queen or Indian rhinitis plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its potential to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery. The bioactive constituents of Hemigraphis crenata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hemigraphis crenata is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea to treat respiratory issues, or used in the form of a syrup or powder to ease cough and congestion.

This family consists of around 2,965 species that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Hemigraphis crenata is a species of flowering plant that is native to Southeast Asia. It is a member of the genus Hemigraphis, which includes around 50 species of plants.
